sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 12.24 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.12)

statement.latex: Given a ring$R$with identity, the automorphisms of$R[[x]]$sending$x$to$x ( 1 + _{i=1}^{ } a_ix^i )$,$a_i R$, form a group$N(R)$. We know that$N({ Z})$contains a copy of the free group$F_2$of rank 2 and, from work of A.\,Weiss, that$N({ Z}/p{ Z})$contains a copy of every finite$p 0.1ex$-group (but not of${ Z}_{p^{ }})$,$p$a prime. Does$N({ Z}/p{ Z})$contain a copy of~$F_2$? { D.\,L.\,Johnson} Yes, ...

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory I_INFINITE_SEARCH; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
